frameworks.name: resource model of visual working memory
frameworks.one_line: limited memory resource distributed flexibly across items, not slot-bound

frameworks.name: parallel encoding
frameworks.one_line: information from multiple items accumulates simultaneously into memory

frameworks.name: slot-based working memory
frameworks.one_line: fixed item-number capacity model is not supported by the data

core_question: How does visual working memory precision evolve over encoding time and how is it reallocated by attentional cues?
key_assumptions: orientation report tasks isolate WM fidelity
memory resources are continuous and divisible
manipulations: presence and type of post-display flash cue (salient vs goal-relevant)
timing of cue relative to encoding/storage
key_findings: recall variability decreased incrementally with longer exposure
two independent limits fit data: an encoding rate and a storage fidelity ceiling
no evidence for a fixed limit on number of items stored
cues rapidly reallocated memory resources to prioritized items, even post-encoding
interpretations: encoding and storage are dissociable capacity constraints on working memory
limited memory resources can be dynamically and rapidly reallocated by attention
